subject: Sociology
title: The Future of Globalization
description: A micro-lesson that makes The Future of Globalization usable.

hook.text: Pandemics, wars, and nationalism test globalization. Is the world pulling apart or finding new ways to connect?

buildup.text: Some scholars predict deglobalization as nations prioritize self-sufficiency and shorten supply chains.

discovery.text: Others see reglobalization: new connections forming around digital economies, green energy, and regional blocs.

twist.text: Technology ensures global connection will continue. The question isn't whether but how and for whose benefit.

climax.text: The future of globalization depends on choices: will it serve markets alone, or build a more just world?

punchline.text: The world stays connected; the terms are negotiable.

quiz.question: What is the key question about globalization's future?
options: Whether it will serve markets alone or build a more just world
